summary: Iteration 2 backend tests PASSED 18/18 against public EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. Focused on the two new endpoints (GET /api/reports/comparison and GET /api/reports/export/json) plus custom-category-icon round-trip, with a quick regression sanity pass on /auth/me, transactions CRUD, /reports/summary, and /reports/export/excel|pdf. Comparison endpoint math verified deterministically by seeding Jan-2025 (prev) + Feb-2025 (cur) tx for User A: income/expense/balance, delta and delta_pct all correct; categories array sorted desc by abs(delta); 0-prev case returns delta_pct=null for both delta.income_pct/delta.expense_pct and per-category delta_pct (verified with 2019-03 vs empty 2019-02). Cross-year previous month resolution validated (year=2026 month=1 -> prev=2025-12). Default invocation (no params) returns current month vs prior month. JSON backup export returns content-type application/json with attachment Content-Disposition, valid JSON shape {exported_at, user, categories, transactions}, transactions match what was inserted, no user_id or voice_note_base64 leakage, and User B's export does not contain User A's tx (data isolation enforced). POST /api/categories with icon='smartphone' persists and is returned by GET /api/categories; omitted icon defaults to 'tag'. Both new endpoints correctly reject missing or bogus Bearer with 401.

minor.issue: pct() uses b<=0 to short-circuit, which also returns null when b is negative (rare but possible for balance comparisons — though only income/expense pcts use it, which can never be negative, so functionally safe).
exported_at is naive .isoformat() via datetime.now(timezone.utc); fine, but consumer code parsing must tolerate '+00:00' (handled in test by replace Z).

action_items: No backend fixes required. New endpoints behave per spec.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py still 950+ lines; iteration 1 recommendation to split into routers still stands.
@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI; migrate to lifespan handler (carried over from iteration 1).
Comparison endpoint uses two sequential _aggregate_for_month calls — fine for current month sizes but could be parallelised with asyncio.gather for very large datasets.
seed_data_creation: Reused session-scoped seed from conftest.py (User A user_test_asatidz / token test_token_asatidz_v1 and User B user_test_asatidzah / token test_token_asatidzah_v1, each with 10 default categories). Test transactions are created inside tests using POST /api/transactions and deleted in fixture teardown, so the DB stays clean for the next iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both seeded users still available. Use EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from frontend/.env as the base. iter2 test file is a good template for future endpoint additions — create transactions via POST then assert via the new endpoint, and always assert delta_pct null path when prev=0.
